title: dianthus basalticus
intro: Dianthus basalticus, also known as the Basalt Pink or Mountain Pink, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a type of wildflower that is native to the Mediterranean region and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Dianthus basalticus include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Dianthus basalticus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to promote healing and relieve symptoms.
